source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Finger Injury during Casing Handling

context.region: Gulf of Mexico, Eugene Island Block 208J

actions_taken: Injured party freed by lifting casing joint with air hoist
Immediate medical treatment by rig medic
Air evacuation to LSU Medical Center
Surgical amputation of distal phalanx of right index finger
Assignment to light duty work post-recovery
event.summary: An Ensco Floorhand sustained a severe finger injury on 14 February 2014 at 14:45 hours while assisting with picking up 7-inch casing joints at the v-door area on the Ensco 90 jack-up rig. The worker installed a lift nubbin in casing joint #2 and tightened it with a valve bar. When tightening the nubbin, casing joint #2 slid down the cat walk, pinning the worker's right index finger between the valve bar and the end of casing joint #3. The finger was nearly amputated at the first knuckle joint. The worker was evacuated to LSU Medical Center where surgical amputation of the distal phalanx was required. The probable cause was attributed to the casing joint not being supported by the back stop, allowing it to slip.
recommendations: Issue safety alert to industry regarding casing handling incidents
Refer incident to Potential Incident of Noncompliance (PINC) team to develop guidance specifically addressing pipe and casing handling operations at v-door areas on rigs
key_phrases: worker not assigned to verify support

hazards.name: Unsupported casing joints on cat walk
Pinch points created by slipping casing
Inadequate spacing of casing joints in v-door

threats.name: Casing joint slips down cat walk due to missing back stop support
Worker hand/finger positioned in pinch point during casing tightening

consequences.name: Crush injury to worker's right index finger
Surgical amputation of finger distal phalanx
Restricted work case and lost productivity

controls.name: Cat walk back stop to support casing joints
controls.barrier_role: Prevent casing joint from slipping down cat walk
controls.lod_basis: Primary engineering control to prevent hazard

supporting_text: The probable cause of the finger injury was attributed to a casing joint slipping down the cat walk because it was not supported by the back stop.

controls.name: Worker assignment to verify casing joint support
controls.barrier_role: Ensure all casing joints are properly supported before operations
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to verify engineering barrier is in place

supporting_text: a worker was not assigned to verify that all casing joints were supported by the cat walk back stop

controls.name: Worker hazard recognition and awareness of pinch points
controls.barrier_role: Identify and avoid pinch points during casing handling
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control relying on worker situational awareness

supporting_text: workers did not recognize that a casing joint was not supported by the cat walk back stop

controls.name: Hazard analysis for casing handling operations
controls.barrier_role: Identify and document pinch point hazards in v-door casing operations
controls.lod_basis: Foundational administrative control for hazard identification

supporting_text: the hazard analysis did not address potential pinch points created by slipping casing joints

controls.name: Adequate spacing of casing joints in v-door area
controls.barrier_role: Reduce pinch point hazard through proper layout and spacing
controls.lod_basis: Engineering design control to minimize pinch point exposure

supporting_text: inadequate spacing of casing joints in the v-door

controls.name: Job planning and safe work procedures for casing handling
controls.barrier_role: Establish and communicate safe work procedures for casing operations
controls.lod_basis: Foundational administrative control for work planning

supporting_text: employees not following safe work practices

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
